columns.description: ACTUAL SPECIFIC columns for tracking the entities the user wants to research (e.g., if tracking AI companies: 'Company Name', 'Website', 'Is Hiring for AI?'). DO NOT create meta-columns like 'Table Name', 'Column Name', 'Description', 'Format' - those are for describing tables, not for tracking the actual entities.

name.description: SPECIFIC column name for the entity being tracked (e.g., 'Company Name', 'Paper Title', 'Product Name') - NOT generic like 'Entity Name' or 'Table Name'

description.description: What data this SPECIFIC column contains for the entity (e.g., 'Name of the AI company', 'URL of company website')

format.description: Data format (String, Number, Boolean, URL, Date, etc.)

importance.description: Column importance level. Use 'ID' for identification columns (simple identifiers like names, dates, URLs). Use 'RESEARCH' for columns requiring validation.

validation_strategy.description: How to validate this column's data (required for research columns)

description.description: Clear description of what entities to find

requirements.description: Row requirements (minimum 1) - can be hard (dealbreakers) or soft (preferences). Bias toward soft requirements.

type.description: Hard = absolute dealbreaker, Soft = preference that improves scores

requirements_notes.description: Overall guidance about what makes a good row

default_included_domains.description: Optional: Default domains to focus on for searches (authoritative sources)

default_excluded_domains.description: Optional: Default domains to exclude from searches (defaults: youtube.com, reddit.com unless specifically needed)

subdomains.description: 2-10 subdomains for parallel row discovery with search queries and targets

focus.description: Detailed description of this subdomain's focus area

search_queries.description: Search queries specific to this subdomain that yield MULTIPLE entities

target_rows.description: Number of candidate rows to find for this subdomain (allows small allocations for niche subdomains)

discovered_list_url.description: Optional: URL of authoritative list/database where candidates can be found (e.g., NIH RePORTER, Wikipedia list, Crunchbase). Leave empty if no single authoritative source exists.

candidates.description: Optional: 3-10 actual example candidates discovered from authoritative lists, with their primary ID values (e.g., ['Anthropic', 'OpenAI'] for companies or ['John Andrews - USC', 'Sarah Davis - MIT'] for researchers). Must be REAL entities found via web search, not made-up examples.

included_domains.description: Optional: Override default included domains for this specific subdomain

excluded_domains.description: Optional: Override default excluded domains for this specific subdomain

tablewide_research.description: Overall research context and objectives for this table

sample_rows.description: Optional: 5-15 example rows from background research starting tables that can be used immediately for QC review. Each row should have ID column values filled in. These rows will be merged with rows from discovery phase (with discovery phase taking precedence for duplicates based on model quality).

id_values.description: ID column values for this row (e.g., {'Company Name': 'Anthropic', 'Website': 'anthropic.com'})

source.description: Which starting table this row came from (e.g., 'Forbes AI 50 2024', 'NIH RePORTER Dementia Research')

match_score.description: Confidence score 0-1 for how well this row matches requirements (default 0.8 for curated list entries)

model_used.description: Model that provided this row (use 'column_definition' for rows from this phase)

complete_rows.description: Optional: ONLY provide this if (1) the list of rows is obvious/exhaustive (e.g., 'countries in Africa', 'planets in solar system') OR (2) background research found a reliable starting table that perfectly matches user needs (JUMP START mode). When provided, row discovery and QC will be SKIPPED entirely. DO NOT use this for open-ended discovery tasks (e.g., 'AI companies', 'research papers on topic X').

skip_row_discovery.description: Set to true to skip row discovery and QC phases entirely. Use when: (1) rows are obvious/complete and don't require web research, OR (2) a reliable starting table from background research is a perfect match (JUMP START).

skip_rationale.description: REQUIRED when skip_row_discovery=true. Explain why row discovery can be skipped. Examples: 'List of countries in Africa is exhaustive and well-known', 'References are from provided paper - complete enumeration possible', 'JUMP START: Forbes AI 50 2024 list from background research perfectly matches user request for top AI companies - directly copying all 50 rows with company names and descriptions'.

mode.description: Mode type: 'complete_enumeration' for well-known finite lists (countries, planets, etc.), 'jump_start' for directly copying rows from a reliable starting table found in background research.

rows.description: Complete list of ALL rows. For complete_enumeration: must be exhaustive (all countries, all planets, etc.). For jump_start: directly copy ALL rows from the starting table with as many columns as available in source. Must be REAL entities in reasonable order (alphabetical, chronological, or logical).

id_values.description: ID column values for this row (e.g., {'Country': 'Nigeria', 'Capital': 'Abuja'}). For jump_start mode, include ALL columns available from the starting table, not just ID columns.

research_values.description: Optional: Research column values already available from the starting table (jump_start mode only). Include any data columns that were in the source table and map to your defined columns.

source.description: Source of this row. For jump_start mode, cite the specific starting table (e.g., 'Forbes AI 50 2024', 'NIH RePORTER grant database', 'Wikipedia list of African countries').

match_score.description: Confidence score 0-1 for this row. Default 1.0 for complete enumerations from reliable sources, 0.9+ for jump_start from authoritative starting tables.
